title: Escalation to operator authority — production deployment approval
description: A concrete example showing the escalation path when an agent encounters a governance decision that requires operator authority. Illustrates what the agent does, what the operator does, and the receipt trail.

scenario.context: An autonomous agent has prepared a production deployment for a public repository. The governance manifest requires operator approval for production releases.
scenario.trigger: Agent completes pre-deployment checks and reaches the 'approval' gate, which requires 'governance-owner' role — a role the agent does not hold.

agentActions.detect: Agent evaluates gate policy and identifies that 'approval' gate requires 'governance-owner' role.
agentActions.pause: Agent halts the deployment workflow. No production mutation occurs.
agentActions.gather: Agent collects context for the operator: change summary, test results, security scan results, and the specific gate that triggered escalation.
agentActions.propose: Agent posts a proposal receipt with all gathered context and a recommended action ('approve deployment').
agentActions.notify: Agent sends escalation notification to the operator via the configured escalation channel.
agentActions.wait: Agent enters a waiting state. It does not retry, bypass, or self-approve.

operatorActions.review: Operator reviews the proposal receipt, test results, and security scan.
operatorActions.decide: Operator issues an explicit decision: 'approve', 'request_change', or 'reject'.
operatorActions.receipt: Operator's decision is recorded as an approval receipt (or rejection receipt) with operator identity and timestamp.

agentActionsAfterDecision.onApprove: Agent proceeds with the deployment, recording a release receipt upon completion.
agentActionsAfterDecision.onRequestChange: Agent implements the requested changes and re-enters the proposal phase.
agentActionsAfterDecision.onReject: Agent halts the workflow, records a rejection receipt, and does not retry without a new proposal.

receiptTrail.content: Deployment proposal with change summary, test results, and security scan
Escalation notification to operator — gate 'approval' requires 'governance-owner' role
Operator decision: approve / request_change / reject, with identity and timestamp

agentDoesNot: Self-approve a gate that requires operator authority
Retry after rejection without a new proposal
Modify the governance manifest to change gate requirements
operatorDoesNot: Delegate approval authority to the agent via ad-hoc instruction (requires manifest change)

nonGoals: This example does not introduce canon.
This example does not create new HUMMBL/BaseN terminology.
This example does not modify operator-authority surfaces.
